rows.notes: Clear centred year at top of page in large script. No monetary columns present; unambiguous.
Clear centred section title in large cursive. No amounts on this line.

Description reading uncertain (handwritten cursive); read as 'Civitati Avon'. Monetary columns (4/14/0) are clearly aligned to ruling lines and validate (shillings 0-19, pence 0-11). Chose these numeric values based on neat alignment with other rows.

Description letters faint and ambiguous; transcribed as 'Annae Leetings' to reflect appearance. Amount reads as a leading dot (no pounds) then 1 s. 8 d. Alignment with vertical column lines is reasonable but description uncertainty lowers confidence.
Description read as 'Pate Mr. Burs.' (the abbreviation 'Mr.' and 'Burs.' visible). Amount shown as no pounds (dot), 18s 4d — digits are dark and well aligned; shillings/pence within valid ranges.
Description likely 'Coll. Comm. Annin.' (college/committee abbreviation) but wording is legible enough. Amount 4/12/6 is clear, digits well aligned to column lines and validate.
Text reads clearly as 'Coll. Balliol'. Amount appears as no pounds then 12s 6d (leading dot). Numerals and alignment are unambiguous.
Description with abbreviations 'D. Jo: Bapt.' is legible. Amount reads as 8s 2d with no pounds; digits are clear and align with column guides.
Description uncertain in final letters (read as 'Adif.'); amount 10/0/0 is clearly written, bold, and aligned. Letter ambiguity reduces confidence slightly.
Description appears to be 'Sanitori' or similar (handwriting a little indistinct). Amount 1/14/8 is legible and aligned; numeric confidence higher than text.
Description 'Public Cler. ad F. Natis. 1830' readable in cursive. Amount 10/10/0 is clear and aligns with the pounds/shillings/pence columns.
Continuation line (leading dash) referencing 1831; text 'cid ad F.D. Jo. Bapt.' visible. Amount 10/10/0 matches alignment of previous similar entry.
Description slightly abbreviated/Latin ('Concionatori ad F. ... 1830'); read as 'Concionatori ad F. Conserr. 1830'. Amount 2/2/0 is clear and within valid ranges.
Follow-on line (dash) referencing 'Pasch. 1831' — text legible. Amount 2/2/0 clearly aligned with other small entries.
Name 'Vicarius Morton' and year 1831 clearly legible. Amount 25/0/0 is prominent and neatly aligned; very high confidence.
Continuation line for same officer (dash) referencing 'S. Mich. 1831'. Amount 25/0/0 matches alignment and is clearly written.

rows.description: Vicar. S. Newton. ad F. Ann. 1831
rows.notes: Text 'Vicar. S. Newton. ad F. Ann. 1831' is legible. Amount 20/0/0 is clear and aligns with the pounds column.
Continuation (dash) referencing 'S. Mich. 1831' again; amount 20/0/0 clearly written and aligned.

rows.description: Assessd Taxes for the Lodgings - April 1831
rows.notes: Description reads 'Assessd Taxes for the Lodgings - April 1831'. Pence column shows '9' with a small trailing loop interpreted as 'ob' (halfpenny) → 0.5; alignment is consistent with other rows.
Abbreviated 'Do. - Do.' (ditto) for October 1831. Pence again shows '9' with a halfpenny mark similar to the April line; digits align neatly.
Description reads clearly as 'Paving & Lighting Tax' (handwriting legible). Amount 3/7/6 visible and well aligned to ruling lines.

rows.description: Land Tax for the Stables yard
rows.notes: Description legible; amount appears to be 1/7/6 but the tens digit in pounds/shillings area is faint — could be 1 or possibly blank; chosen 1/7/6 based on alignment with neighbouring entries.

rows.description: River Water from the City works
rows.notes: Clear description 'River Water from the City works'. Amount 5/5/0 is dark and aligned to the pounds/shillings/pence columns.
Description 'Insurance of College' clear. Amount 45/15/0 is prominent and easily read; numeric alignment very clear.
Text reads 'College Gardiner 1st Do. to Xmas 1830' (gardener spelling consistent with original). Amount 8/3/6 is clear and aligned.
Continuation line for gardener payments ('2nd to Do. Jan 1831'). Amount reads 9/18/9; digits are clear though the 18s is close to the 20 boundary (but still valid).
Line shows '3rd' and a month abbreviation that reads like 'Mid' (mid-year) — transcription of text uncertain. Amount 14/11/9 is clear and well aligned; uncertainty mainly in the month/word.
Continuation '4th to Mich. 1831' (Michaelmas). Amount 8/4/6 is legible and aligns with the pounds/shillings/pence columns.
Final total at bottom right of page ('£252 10 4') is very clear, heavily written and aligns perfectly with the column guides. Used as alignment reference for column detection.
